$schema: see scripts/lanes.mjs — `node scripts/lanes.mjs check` validates this file
$why: Two agents now: B-engine builds, D-verify dogfoods the running product and files what it finds. That is the cookbook's evaluator/optimizer split — the lane that verifies owns no source file, because a lane that fixes what it finds stops looking once it finds something it can fix. Ownership lives here rather than in prose because prose forked between worktrees and four duplicate commits landed anyway. `node scripts/lanes.mjs check` validates this file.
$shared: Globs either lane may edit, because coordination itself lives in them. Append, never rewrite another lane's entry. build-tracker.json is shared now that C-data is closed: B-engine marks rows it ships, D-verify files rows dogfooding surfaces. Edit the row you own the truth about, never rewrite the other's.

lanes.role: PM-2 · the builder — all product code; claims files as it takes them
lanes.$queueNote: scan-cannot-complete-slow-model closed to shipped on 2026-07-23 after A-view drove it live (2002s scan completed, first past the ~307s ceiling, 10 findings, Services "Opportunities (10)"). Queue empty. agent-config enforcement (2cc0de6) is landed and awaiting D-verify drive #2 — not queued here because it is verification, not build. Two open criticals remain: prose-credentials-reach-sinks (a ruling on the frozen §5 pattern set — needs a false-positive corpus, not a patch) and parked-anthropic-zero-balance (the account is at $0 — a billing action, not code).

lanes.role: dogfoods the running product on localhost, files what it finds, and does not fix it — owns no product source on purpose
lanes.$note: Owning nothing is the point. A lane that both builds and signs off on its own work is not verification. It files findings against the tracker and against whichever lane owns the file; it does not fix them.

$closed: A-view (ridley) and C-data (Kuma2) were closed by the user on 2026-07-23. Removed rather than left listed: a board showing lanes nobody is running is the stale record this system exists to prevent. Their files are unclaimed and B-engine, as the only builder, may take them — claiming each one as it does, not in bulk. Their history is in git.
